title: Add rival-stop reaction features to model cover-the-undercut behavior
summary: Teams often pit reactively because a nearby rival already pitted or threatened an undercut.
rationale: Teams often pit reactively because a nearby rival already pitted or threatened an undercut. Prior ideas covered pack density and clean air, but not the direct contagion pattern where the first mover triggers a response on the next lap.
implementation_hint: Within each race, add causal counts for how many cars pitted on the previous 1-2 laps in the same stint/compound band, nearby position band, and same latent regime. Add flags for whether the immediately adjacent position pitted, whether the driver just became exposed to an undercut, and whether a prior pit wave is still propagating.
expected_effect: Should help on the sharp next-lap spikes that occur after one frontrunner or nearby rival blinks first.
risk: Without actual time gaps, broad counts may be noisy. Keep strata narrow, smooth historical rates out-of-fold, and prefer previous-lap reactions over long lookbacks.
